common.save_hint: Mobile signal is weak along the route due to the mountains. Tap Share → Add to Home Screen to save this guide to your phone.

house.title: Finding Greg's House
house.subtitle: Step-by-step walking guide
house.tldr: 15-minute walk from Amalfi. Follow the coastal road through the tunnel to Atrani, then navigate through the charming streets up to the house.
house.porters: Have heavy luggage? Look for Amalfi Porters — small golf carts right at the port and bus stop. For about €20 they’ll drive you to the house area and can carry your bags up the ~200 steps to the apartment.

awesome.title: Finding the Meeting Point
awesome.subtitle: Step-by-step walking guide
awesome.tldr: Walk from Amalfi through the coastal tunnel to Atrani. The meeting point is just past the tunnel exit at the Atrani center.
awesome.from_atrani_desc: The meeting point is right at the tunnel exit in Atrani. If you're already in Atrani, it's just a short walk.

01.caption: You’ve just arrived at the Amalfi ferry port. Climb the short flight of stairs from the dock — you’ll see the ‘Porto di Amalfi’ sign pointing the way up.
01.alt: Stairs at Porto di Amalfi ferry dock with Porto di Amalfi sign

02.caption: You’re now on the Amalfi harbor promenade, just a few steps from the bus stop. Looking ahead, this is the direction you need to go. Don’t walk back toward the sea — there’s only a lighthouse behind you.
02.alt: Amalfi harbor promenade with lantern and imbarco boarding sign

03.caption: Walk along the Amalfi promenade. The bus stop is just a short walk ahead.
03.alt: Amalfi promenade with parked cars and sea view

04.caption: This is the Amalfi bus stop. Buses from Salerno, Sorrento, Agerola, and other Amalfi Coast towns stop here, so it’s always busy. From this point, head quickly in the direction shown.
04.alt: Amalfi SITA bus stop with bus shelter and toilette sign

05.caption: Continue along the coastal promenade. You’ll pass several beautiful restaurants with stunning sea views.
05.alt: Amalfi coastal promenade with Pizza Sirena restaurant and pine trees

06.caption: This is your turn. If you keep going straight, you’ll eventually reach Atrani too — but I strongly recommend turning left here. It’s the shorter, safer route.
06.alt: Amalfi street with flower pots and ornate lantern at the turn point

07.caption: Cross the pedestrian crossing and turn left. You’ll reach Café Déjà Vu, and next to it there’s a tunnel. This tunnel is the shortest, safest, and fastest way to Atrani.
07.alt: Crosswalk near Café Déjà Vu and the pedestrian tunnel to Atrani

08.caption: Here you can see Café Déjà Vu on the left, and to the right of it — the tunnel to Atrani. Walk straight in.
08.alt: Café Déjà Vu with the tunnel entrance to Atrani on the right

09.caption: As you enter the tunnel, you’ll see gates directly ahead. They’re usually open during the day — it’s a path to the town cemetery. You don’t need to go through them, just keep walking along the tunnel.
09.alt: Inside the tunnel with cemetery gates and green traffic light ahead

10.caption: Continue through the tunnel. You can see light from the exit up ahead.
10.alt: Inside the tunnel walking toward the light at the exit

11.caption: Just before the exit, you’ll notice a public restroom on the right side and the exit from a parking garage.
11.alt: Tunnel near exit with public restroom sign on the right

12.caption: You’ve exited the tunnel. On the left is the Luna Rossa parking entrance, and on the right the road continues toward Atrani.
12.alt: Luna Rossa parking entrance and road toward Atrani

13.caption: Keep walking straight along the road. You’re heading toward Atrani now.
13.alt: Il Portale di Atrani — the grand stone archway entrance to Atrani

01.caption: You’re in Atrani! Right before the next tunnel, turn right and walk through the restaurant area. You’ll see Zaccaria Ristorante on your right.
01.alt: Zaccaria Ristorante with tunnel on left and sea view terrace on right

02.caption: Walk through the restaurant terrace — almost everyone takes this path. It’s a beautiful spot on the cliff with amazing sea views. Keep going and start heading down the stairs.
02.alt: Clifftop restaurant terrace with panoramic sea and mountain views

03.caption: Descend the stairs — it’s just about two floors down.
03.alt: View from above showing stairs descending toward the coastal road

04.caption: Keep following the road as it takes you gently downhill.
04.alt: Coastal road with parked cars and sea view in Atrani

05.caption: You’re practically in Atrani now. Just keep walking along the road.
05.alt: Panoramic view of Atrani from the coastal road with cliffs and turquoise sea

06.caption: Walk down to the parking area and continue straight ahead. Head into the first arch you see.
06.alt: Atrani parking area with arched passages and sea view

07.caption: In the first arch, you’ll find a tunnel passage. Walk straight through it.
07.alt: Large brick arched passages in old Atrani

08.caption: Walk through the tunnel and continue straight.
08.alt: Beautiful brick archway portico in Atrani with cobblestone path

09.caption: As you exit, you’ll see the main church of Atrani — San Salvatore de’ Birecto — with its clock tower and town hall. Great photo spot! The town square is to the right, cafés and restaurants to the left.
09.alt: San Salvatore de’ Birecto church with clock tower in Atrani

10.caption: Go straight into the next tunnel passage. You’ll see shops on the left and a fish shop on the right. Keep walking straight through.
10.alt: Arched tunnel passage in Atrani with lantern and bar sign

11.caption: Walk until you reach the large inscription on the wall. Continue slightly uphill — you’re only about 50 meters away now.
11.alt: Atrani street with a large literary inscription on the wall

12.caption: Your next landmark is Ristorante A’ Paranza. Don’t be surprised — it’s one of the most exclusive and delicious restaurants in town. Just keep walking straight past it.
12.alt: A’ Paranza restaurant sign on a quiet Atrani street

13.caption: Continue just a bit further past the restaurant.
13.alt: Quiet Atrani street with traditional buildings and mountains

14.caption: Look for the fruit shop — it may be open with fruit displayed outside, or closed with brown doors. Right past it you’ll see a small green door and immediately a very narrow turn to the right. Don’t miss it!
14.alt: Atrani street with fruit shop and narrow passage ahead

15.caption: Here’s the very narrow turn to the right — you must go in here! If the Amalfi Porters drop you off, you’ll be right across from the fruit shop. This is exactly where you need to turn.
15.alt: Very narrow archway entrance with stairs and green door

16.caption: Enter the small gallery tunnel. You’ll see a green door ahead — turn right immediately from it.
16.alt: Narrow tunnel passage with green door and right turn

17.caption: Walk through this narrow tunnel toward the light at the end.
17.alt: Narrow Via del Tempio tunnel with light at the end

18.caption: Right in front of you are about 10 steps. Climb up — there’s a green door on the left.
18.alt: Stone steps leading up with green door and Comune di Atrani marking

19.caption: This is the view after you climb the steps — you’re right at our house!
19.alt: Small courtyard at Traversa Dragone in Atrani with plants

20.caption: Walk to the green door. You’ll see a doorbell on the left — press it and we’ll come meet you right away. Welcome, you’ve arrived!
20.alt: Green entrance door of Greg’s House with doorbell in a stone passage

01.caption: Start at the Atrani bus stop in the main square. You'll see the bus shelter with the mountains behind.
01.alt: Atrani bus stop and main piazza with mountain backdrop

02.caption: From the bus stop, head toward the village center. Follow the road downhill.
02.alt: Road from bus stop heading toward Atrani center

03.caption: Continue along the road. You'll see the village buildings ahead.

04.caption: Keep walking along the main road. Look for the turn-off into the village.
04.alt: Main road through Atrani near the village entrance

05.caption: Follow the road as it leads into the village streets.
05.alt: Road leading into the narrow Atrani streets

06.caption: Enter the village streets. The path becomes narrower here.
06.alt: Narrow village entrance from the bus stop direction

07.caption: Continue into the narrow streets. You'll join the main path to the house from here.
07.alt: Narrow street connecting to the main route through Atrani

01.caption: At the tunnel exit, you'll see the road leading into Atrani with directional signs ahead.
01.alt: Atrani tunnel exit with road signs and town view

02.caption: This is the meeting point! You've arrived. Look for Greg nearby.
02.alt: The meeting point at the Atrani center
